name: Component.Filter.View

unions.name: CssClasses
unions.comment: The CSS classes used in this view

values.name: view
values.comment: Displays all filters in the model side-by-side
values.type: Addresses a -> Address Action -> Model b c -> Html

name: Component.Filter.Update
comment:
Our API for a filtering component
@docs Action, Addresses, update

unions.name: Action
unions.comment:
The actions defined here can be considered our external API
The top level component will be able to trigger these actions,
as well as the local component

aliases.name: Addresses
aliases.comment:
we demand that we get an addresses record with an entry for our filter called "filter"
this allows us to compose other addresses together and yet only have a single
atom at the top level

values.name: update
values.comment:
Takes the addresses record, the action, and the model
Runs the defined action on the model
values.type: Addresses a -> Action -> Model b c -> ( Model b c, Effects Action )

aliases.name: FilterField
aliases.comment: Each filter field has a bunch of properties useful for displaying data
aliases.type: { name : String, isDisabled : Bool, isChecked : Bool, isVisible : Bool, value : String, text : String, type_ : a }

aliases.name: Model
aliases.comment:
Demand that the model has a filter property. This means you can either use
a top level model like
```
type alias Model = { filters : [] }
```
or
```
type alias Model = { filterA : { filters [] } }
```
